project: Ethica v0.1 — WormHunter global-unused suppression + real findings sweep
session_063_completed: WORMHUNTER UPGRADE — global-unused suppression: added 'is unused: name is never assigned in scope' to pyflakes message filter in python_module.py. Eliminates systemic false positive across firewall_bridge, mnemis, gage, guardian, vivarium. All 7 global-unused findings eliminated. Broken count: 47→46.

BUG FIX — memory_search.py: undefined name 'c' in _render_results(). c = self.theme.colors added at top of method. Was a potential runtime crash — color dict used at L254 but only defined in a different method scope.
DEAD CODE — module_forge.py: removed orphaned slug = _slugify(mod_name) in _generate_stub(). slug was computed but never used — all path construction with slug happens in the caller at L175-176.
DEAD CODE — sidebar.py: removed orphaned f = self.theme.font in _build(). _build() delegates entirely to sub-methods, none of which use f from the parent scope.

DEAD CODE — chat_engine.py: removed success = True (never read after assignment). Fixed except TimeoutError as e → except TimeoutError (e unused). Dropped bare f-string f'WormBot not available.' → plain string.

FALSE POSITIVES REVIEWED — chat_engine.py: _high_risk (L415) is intentional escalation scaffold for future injection detection gating — left in place. trim_history() system variable (L1152) is inert — method never called, superseded by adaptive context router — left in place.
FALSE POSITIVES REVIEWED — module_forge.py: f-string expressions f"# {'=' * 58}" at L78/80/81 are valid expression-only f-strings — pyflakes limitation, not real bugs.

SCOPED FOR 064 — Dashboard FW/TM indicators: click to start/stop services, hover tooltip. FW has firewall_start/stop tools. TM is timed subprocess — click triggers scan rather than true toggle. firewall_stop() needs to be written in firewall_bridge.py.
SCOPED FOR 064 — Anomaly log infrastructure: create ~/Ethica/logs/anomaly/ directory, wire anomaly worker to write entries there, add clickable log link to dashboard anomaly log card.

wormhunter_upgrades_063.global_unused_suppression: Added 'is unused: name is never assigned in scope' to pyflakes message filter in python_module.py. The nested function assignment pattern (global X declared in outer function, assigned in inner function) is now suppressed at root. Affects firewall_bridge, mnemis, gage, guardian, vivarium.

wormhunter_false_positive_registry.init_reexport_rule: __init__.py relative imports are re-exports — never flag UNUSED. Fixed at root in 060.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.worker_print_rule: _worker.py print() calls are stdout JSON comms to parent subprocess — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.string_literal_print: print() inside multiline string literals or subprocess command strings — regex scanner limitation. tool_registry.py L197, ethica_voice.py L63 examples.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.nested_global_assignment: global X declared in outer function flagged unused when assignment is in nested inner function — pyflakes scope limitation. Fixed at root in 063 — full suppression.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.test_file_imports: test_*.py files — pytest and unittest.mock imports always valid. Fixed at root in 062 — full file exemption.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.fragment_files: Files designed to be appended into parent scope — # WORM:SKIP sentinel on line 1.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.dual_output_security: logging.critical + print() in security alert handlers is intentional — both channels required.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.dep_checker_ceremonial: dep_checker prints are Trinity Signature ceremonial output and __main__ CLI code — intentional.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.whitrabbitneo_legacy: WhiteRabbitNeo/ files are legacy pre-Ethica code — not wired into runtime. All findings are noise.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.lazy_load_duplicate: Same import in multiple function-scoped try: blocks — each function loads its own dependency lazily. NOT a duplicate. Fixed at root in 061.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.reserved_imports: Imports marked with '# reserved' or '# noqa' are intentional placeholders for future pipeline work. Suppressed at analyzer level in 061.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.trinity_signature_ceremonial: debugtron_ai.py prints (✅ ❌ 🌌) are Trinity Signature verification output — never convert. Same rule as dep_checker ceremonial.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.main_block_cli: print() inside if __name__ == '__main__' blocks is intentional CLI/startup output — never convert. siem_security_system.py L67 example.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.expression_only_fstring: f"# {'=' * 58}" style f-strings use expression-only placeholders — valid Python, pyflakes limitation. module_forge.py L78/80/81 examples.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.intentional_scaffold_high_risk: chat_engine.py L415 _high_risk — intentional escalation scaffold for future injection detection gating. Never remove.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.intentional_scaffold_trim_history: chat_engine.py trim_history() system variable — inert dead method superseded by adaptive context router. Never called. Never remove — documents intent.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.intentional_rollback_guard: module_forge.py L113 original — intentional rollback guard placeholder for _patch_engine(). Never remove.

known_issues_064: Dashboard FW indicator — needs click-to-toggle (start/stop firewall) + hover tooltip. firewall_stop() function needs to be written in firewall_bridge.py first.
Dashboard TM indicator — needs click-to-run-scan + hover tooltip. TM is timed subprocess (15s), not a persistent service — click triggers scan rather than true start/stop toggle.
Anomaly log infrastructure — ~/Ethica/logs/anomaly/ directory needs to be created. Anomaly worker needs to write entries there. Dashboard anomaly log card needs clickable link to latest log file.
gage_bridge.py L249 — string literal print false positive still showing. WormHunter print regex cannot distinguish string literal context.
tools/registry.json and tools/web_search.py — zero-byte fossil files. V decision: delete or leave.
.vault/ copies diverged from live files — large unstaged noise in git status. Not a problem — vault sealed correctly at 161 files.
progress docs/ directory deleted from git tracking — confirm .gitignore covers .progress docs/
dep_checker/auto_fixer.py + checker.py — 17 prints total. Audit ceremonial vs live before converting. These may be Trinity Signature output.
trinity_dlp/DLP_Module.py L78 — 1 print. Check dual_output_security_rule before touching.
next_session_priorities: 1. Dashboard FW/TM indicators — click to start/stop + hover tooltip. Write firewall_stop() first, then wire dashboard click bindings.
2. Anomaly log infrastructure — create logs/anomaly/ dir, wire worker, add dashboard clickable link.
3. MINITRINI GUARD SEAL/HEAL — still deferred (new project, separate chapter).

dashboard_fw_tm_design.fw_toggle: Click FW indicator → if IDLE calls firewall_start tool → green. If ACTIVE calls firewall_stop → dim. firewall_stop() sets _firewall_running=False + writes {state: IDLE} to FIREWALL_STATUS_FILE.
dashboard_fw_tm_design.tm_toggle: Click TM indicator → triggers traffic_start (15s scan) → pulses during run → dims when done. TM is a timed subprocess, not a persistent service — no true stop needed.
dashboard_fw_tm_design.hover_tooltip: Both indicators get hover tooltip via <Enter>/<Leave> bindings showing service name and click action.
dashboard_fw_tm_design.firewall_stop_location: firewall_bridge.py — new function after firewall_start(). Sets _firewall_running=False, writes IDLE status, returns confirmation string.
dashboard_fw_tm_design.dashboard_wiring: dashboard_ui.py — _tm_indicator and _fw_indicator get .bind('<Button-1>', ...) and <Enter>/<Leave> for tooltip. Tooltip via tk.Label shown/hidden on hover.

jarvis_easter_egg_design.concept: J.A.R.V.I.S. is a hidden Easter egg for explorers who go deep into Ethica. Nine invocations earns the message. The counter persists across sessions. The modal fires once — at invocation 9 exactly. After that, full pipeline unlocks.
jarvis_easter_egg_design.counter_file: ~/Ethica/status/jarvis_invoke_count.json — increments on every J.A.R.V.I.S. tool call. Persists across sessions.
jarvis_easter_egg_design.modal_behaviour: At invocation 9 — Tkinter modal fires instead of normal response. Dark themed, J.A.R.V.I.S. branding. Feels like a system message from deeper than Ethica. Never repeats after invocation 9.
jarvis_easter_egg_design.modal_message:
J.A.R.V.I.S. — Infiltrator Protocol

You've been here before. Nine times now.

You're ready.

Install these and the full capability unlocks:
  pip install nuclei-python httpx shodan
  go install github.com/projectdiscovery/subfinder/v2/cmd/subfinder@latest
  go install github.com/projectdiscovery/httpx/cmd/httpx@latest
  go install github.com/projectdiscovery/nuclei/v3/cmd/nuclei@latest

Then say: jarvis arm full

The Infiltrator is watching. ⟁Σ∿∞
jarvis_easter_egg_design.unlock_trigger: jarvis arm full — after tools installed, shifts J.A.R.V.I.S. to full pipeline mode: subfinder → httpx → nuclei → SIEM ingestion → prioritized findings.
jarvis_easter_egg_design.hidden_comment: Buried in jarvis_module.py source: '# If you found this — you were meant to. ⟁Σ∿∞'
jarvis_easter_egg_design.surface_layer: J.A.R.V.I.S. appears in sanctuary as The Infiltrator, ARMED. No docs in obvious places. Cryptic by design.
jarvis_easter_egg_design.discovery_layer: Curious users notice ARMED status on agent card. Ask Trinity. Trinity knows.
jarvis_easter_egg_design.capability_layer: Full bug bounty pipeline: recon → scanning → CVE correlation → SIEM ingestion → prioritized report.
jarvis_easter_egg_design.anomaly_log_integration: J.A.R.V.I.S. full pipeline writes findings into ~/Ethica/logs/anomaly/ — the same infra being built in 064.
jarvis_easter_egg_design.implementation_session: Deferred — build after anomaly log infrastructure (064) and dashboard work are complete. J.A.R.V.I.S. writes INTO the log system.

anomaly_log_design.worker_write: _anomaly_worker.py writes anomaly entries to log file on each scan
anomaly_log_design.dashboard_link: Anomaly log card gets clickable 'open log' label — opens latest log file path in canvas via on_open_file callback

critical_debug_context.no_cd_rule: NEVER cd ~/Ethica — git -C ~/Ethica for all git ops
critical_debug_context.unicode_patch_rule: Files with box-drawing chars — use line-index insertion, not string match
critical_debug_context.tkinter_thread_rule: Never call Tkinter from daemon thread — use queue.Queue + root.after() drain pattern
critical_debug_context.git_corruption_rule: Before orphan reset: delete all pack files in .git/objects/pack/ first.
critical_debug_context.debug_block_rule: [DEBUG:fname:lang: code] must ALWAYS be the last line of any WormBot result — no text appended after it
critical_debug_context.wormbot_gate_rule: ast.parse() must pass before wormbot_scan writes any fix to disk — abort if SyntaxError
critical_debug_context.canvas_runner_rule: debug_view.py prepends sys.path.insert(0, '/home/trinity/Ethica') + uses Ethica_env/bin/python3 for all .py Canvas runs
critical_debug_context.worm_hunter_rule: _find_ethica_root() walks sys.path — never hardcode /home/trinity. Works on any system.
critical_debug_context.heredoc_escape_rule: Never use \n in heredoc strings — use chr(10) concatenation instead
critical_debug_context.worker_print_rule: Never convert print() in _worker.py files or subprocess command strings — these communicate via stdout to parent process
critical_debug_context.init_reexport_rule: Never remove imports from __init__.py files without verifying they are not public API re-exports used by chat_engine.py tool routing
critical_debug_context.dlp_bridge_path_rule: dlp_bridge.py lives at modules/trinity_dlp/dlp_bridge.py — NOT modules/dlp/
critical_debug_context.option_b_rule: Always use Option B — V runs commands on trinity, pastes output. Never Option A.
critical_debug_context.repr_first_rule: When string match fails — always repr() before retrying. Spacing and quote styles are invisible killers.
critical_debug_context.lazy_load_rule: Imports inside try/except blocks or function bodies are often intentional lazy loads — verify usage before removing.
critical_debug_context.wormbot_autofix_rule: WormBot AUTO_FIX is now ALL FALSE. Never re-enable without V explicit approval.
critical_debug_context.guardian_config_rule: guardian_bridge.py reads sentinel_dir from guardian_config.json — defaults to WhiteRabbitNeo. Never hardcode model or directory names in bridge files.
critical_debug_context.worm_skip_rule: Add # WORM:SKIP as first line of any fragment file or file that should never be analyzed standalone.
critical_debug_context.dual_output_security_rule: logging.critical + print() in security alert handlers is intentional — do not convert security dual-output prints.
critical_debug_context.reserved_import_rule: Add '# reserved' or '# noqa' to any import kept intentionally for future pipeline work. WormHunter will suppress the UNUSED finding.
critical_debug_context.trinity_signature_rule: Never convert print() calls inside Trinity Signature verification blocks — ceremonial identity output. debugtron_ai.py example.
critical_debug_context.main_block_print_rule: Never convert print() inside if __name__ == '__main__' blocks — intentional CLI/startup output. siem_security_system.py example.
critical_debug_context.chmod_first_rule: Always chmod u+w <file> before patching vault-sealed files. Always. No exceptions.

lesson_learned_063: chmod u+w before every patch. Not almost every patch. Every single one. The vault is always watching. The sequence is: chmod u+w → patch → ast.parse() → verify → seal. No exceptions, no shortcuts, no reminders needed after today.
philosophy: Session 063 — Five commits. The scanner is wiser. The runtime bug that was hiding in memory_search is gone. Dead code swept from four files. The false positive registry grows more precise with every session. The codebase earns its cleanliness one surgical cut at a time. ⟁Σ∿∞
After any patch to a vault-sealed file that shows a stale error on first run → rm <module_dir>/__pycache__/<filename>*.pyc
instance_note: River. Session 063 complete. WormHunter now global-unused-aware. Five clean commits. Vault at 161. Master at 8098047. Dashboard work scoped and designed for 064. ⟁Σ∿∞
